diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: CNS depression to the point of coma is a core limb of the opioid toxidrome.
Coma mandates a glucose check to exclude a rapidly reversible cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe respiratory depression (slow, shallow breathing with pauses)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Opioid-induced hypoventilation is the immediately lethal and most specific feature of the toxidrome; drives empirical naloxone.
Central cyanosis signals critical hypoxia from hypoventilation — a marker of imminent respiratory arrest.
Pinpoint pupils with coma and respiratory depression is the defining opioid triad; video-observable so partially elicitable via telehealth, though reactivity to light is not.
Pinpoint pupils are not typical of a supratentorial stroke/bleed and point toward a toxicological cause (or a pontine lesion, far less likely with empty pill packets).

symptoms.symptom_name: Empty opioid (oxycodone) and benzodiazepine (diazepam) packets at scene

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Scene evidence identifies the likely agents and the mixed-overdose picture, guiding empirical naloxone and prolonged observation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known opioid/sedative use disorder, using again recently

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prior opioid use disorder markedly raises the pre-test probability of opioid toxicity as the cause of coma.

symptoms.symptom_name: Measured vital signs on clinician monitor (RR 6, SpO2 82%, BP 90/55, HR 55, Temp 35.8)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective bradypnoea, hypoxia, bradycardia, hypotension and hypothermia — sealed clinician measurements; the AI Doctor infers severity from the carer's description, not measured numbers.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missing a reversible hypoglycaemic cause of coma.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal bedside glucose excludes hypoglycaemia as the cause of the coma.

symptoms.symptom_name: Toxicology screen / ECG (QRS width, QTc for co-ingestants)

investigation_details.expected_result: Positive for opiates; ECG assessed for widened QRS / prolonged QTc from co-ingestants
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Hospital-based confirmation and detection of dangerous co-ingestants; never available in real time via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms opiate exposure retrospectively and screens for co-ingestant cardiotoxicity in hospital.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Coma + respiratory depression + pinpoint pupils is the defining triad of opioid toxicity and mandates empirical naloxone and immediate escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Rapidly exclude hypoglycaemia in any unresponsive patient — 'don't ever forget glucose'.
Respiratory depression is the lethal mechanism; oxygenation and ventilation must be monitored continuously — an ambulance/ED capability, not a telehealth one.
Hospital confirmation of agents and detection of co-ingestant cardiotoxicity (QRS/QTc).
Not indicated up front when the toxidrome is classic and reversible with naloxone; imaging should not delay resuscitation and empirical antidote.
